subject: Chemistry
title: The Green Chemistry Movement
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Green Chemistry Movement usable.

hook.text: After decades of disasters, chemists asked: what if we designed reactions that can't produce toxic waste at all?

buildup.text: Paul Anastas and John Warner published 12 principles of Green Chemistry in 1998—a manifesto for safer chemistry.

discovery.text: Key principles: use renewable feedstocks, design for degradation, minimize waste, and avoid toxic solvents.

twist.text: Ibuprofen used to be made in 6 steps with tons of waste. Green chemistry reduced it to 3 steps with minimal waste.

climax.text: Green chemistry now saves industry billions yearly. Prevention is cheaper than cleanup—and far safer.

punchline.text: Design out the danger from the start. Prevention beats cleanup every time.

quiz.question: What is the core idea behind Green Chemistry?
options: Design chemical processes that prevent toxic waste from being created
Use only natural ingredients in all chemical products
Recycle all chemical waste back into useful products
